concerns.text: T031 mandates 'chunked data loading' to satisfy FR-006, but the Plan (T011) and Spec (US-1) explicitly define the memory enforcement mechanism as 'Stratified Random Sampling' triggered at 6.5 GB. T031 introduces a conflicting technical strategy (chunking vs. sampling) without a corresponding amendment in plan.md, violating the Single Source of Truth and potentially weakening the defined constraint preservation logic.

T011 introduces a hard failure condition ('limit to a maximum of 5 iterations') for the downsampling loop. The Spec (US-1, Acceptance Scenario 3) only requires 'graceful downsampling' without specifying an iteration limit. This task silently narrows the acceptance criteria by adding a failure mode not authorized by the spec, risking project failure if the dataset requires more aggressive downsampling.

Tasks T029, T030, and T031 are marked as unchecked (not completed) in the task list, yet T027 (Final Report) depends on T029, and T031 is required to satisfy FR-006 per the task description. This creates a logical contradiction where the pipeline cannot complete its defined success criteria (report generation, memory enforcement) because the tasks required to satisfy the spec are marked as optional or incomplete.

T023 defines the 'Failure Boundary' using a Bonferroni-corrected p-value threshold (0.0167) and prioritizes the Wilcoxon test. The Spec (US-3, Acceptance Scenario 3) defines the boundary as 'p-value < 0.05 via paired t-test'. T023 silently tightens the statistical success criteria and changes the test method without a documented amendment in the spec or plan, violating the requirement to preserve the spec's acceptance criteria.
